content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 13, 1991


Mark L. Rowney, General Manager
Mariposa Public Utility District
P.O. Box 494
Mariposa, CA 95338

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-081

Dear Mr. Rowney:

You are seeking advice on behalf of the Mariposa Public
Utility District ("MPUD") Board of Directors ("board") under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹ We provide you with advice as the board's authorized
representative, pursuant to Regulation 18329 (copy enclosed).

The following advice is based upon the facts provided in your
letter of February 7, 1991 and a copy of the district's fire
protection ordinance subsequently provided to us.

The advice provided in this letter is limited to provisions
of the Act. Because the Commission does not provide advice with
respect to past conduct, the following advice is prospective in
its application.² The advice is provided to you in your
representative capacity for the board (an understanding confirmed
with your office by telephone on March 4, 1991) and does not
address potential conflict-of-interest issues concerning other
individuals.

QUESTION

An employee of an insurance agency serves as the district's
volunteer fire chief. His employer currently provides insurance
policies for the district. Are members of the district board

_________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Di-
vision 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² As is discussed infra, you have informed us that the board's
recent action is contingent upon the advice to be provided by the
Commission. For this reason, we understand your request as one
for prospective advice rather than one seeking information of
action already taken by the board.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. A-91-081
Page 2


prohibited from voting to make the position of fire chief a
compensated one?

CONCLUSION

On the basis of the facts given, board members are not
prohibited from voting to make the position of fire chief a
compensated one. The fire chief should seek advice concerning
whether his participation in certain decisions is prohibited under
the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions.

FACTS

Fire protection, water, and sewer services are provided
within specified boundaries in Mariposa County by the Mariposa
Public Utility District. To date, the fire department is staffed
by volunteers. Bill Bondshu, a member of the volunteer fire
department, was appointed to the position of fire chief by the
MPUD directors. Subsequently Mr. Bondshu suggested, and on
January 25, 1991 the MPUD directors conditionally voted, to make
the position of fire chief a compensated one, at a rate of $300
per month without any other district employee benefits. The
board's vote was contingent upon a determination that such an
action would not violate the Act and other statutes.

Mr. Bondshu is a salaried employee at his father's insurance
agency. The agency provides the MPUD with liability and fidelity
bond insurance policies, offering insurance coverage for the
activities of MPUD services, including fire protection.

ANALYSIS

A public official may not make, participate in or use his or
her official position to influence a governmental decision in
which the official knows or has reason to know he or she has a
financial interest. (Section 87100.) A financial interest in a
decision exists if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision
will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its
effect on the public generally, on the official or a member of his
or her immediate family. A financial interest is also present if
the decision will have a specified financial effect on a business
entity in which the official invests or holds a position of
management, on real property in which the official is interested,
or on a source of income or gift to the official. (Section
87103.)

Decision by board members

As public officials (Section 82048), members of the MPUD must
disqualify themselves from any board of directors decisions which
will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect,
distinguishable from the public generally, on them or their
interests.

The effect of a decision is reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that it will occur. While certainty

Our File No. A-91-081
Page 3

is not required, an effect that is merely a possibility is not
reasonably foreseeable. (Downey Cares v. Downey Community
Development Com. (1987) 196 Cal.App.3d 983; In the Thorner (1975)
1 FPPC Ops. 198.) The Act, however, does seek to prevent even the
appearance of a possible conflict of interest. (Witt v. Morrow
(1977) 70 Cal.App.3d 817, 823.)

You have not provided any facts which suggest that a board
member's participation in the vote to make the position of fire
chief a compensated one would result in a financial effect on the
board member as provided for in Section 87103. The relationship
of each board member in an official capacity to the insurance
agency and, through the agency to Mr. Bondshu, is not one to which
any of the foreseeability and materiality criteria of Section
87103 appear to apply. Therefore, absent additional information
to the contrary, we have no basis upon which to conclude that a
board member's participation in the vote to make the position of
fire chief would run contrary to the Act's conflict-of-interest
provisions.

Future decisions by Mr. Bondshu

Whether a particular decision in which Mr. Bondshu as MPUD
fire chief participates would trigger application of the Act's
conflict-of-interest provisions because of his status as an
employee with the agency providing insurance services for the
district will depend on the facts of the decision in question.
Other than noting that one aspect of the Act's foreseeability and
materiality criteria references an official's source of income
(Section 87103(c)), we cannot provide advice on this question.
When appropriate, we suggest that Mr. Bondshu or his authorized
representative seek advice.

I trust this letter has provided you with the assistance you
have sought. If you have any further questions regarding this
matter please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:ken
Enclosure
